title: Level 860

[[File:1130-2.gif|2000px]]
{{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.cdnfonts.com/css/martian-mono'); @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Google+Sans+Code:ital,wght@0,300..800;1,300..800&display=swap'); html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Martian Mono'; } }} {{DISPLAYTITLE:‎Level 860: Abyssal Decay}}
[[File:Vignette.png|2000px]]
...
By now, you've grown accustomed to your new life.
Ever since that day, the same cycle repeats. The Void breaches into yet another level, and what comes after is raw, unfiltered destruction.
You can't count or name how many times you've had to be evacuated to Base Equilibrium for every time this has happened.
So when you heard that there was this peculiar case of a level that refused to collapse, you thought it would be a glimmer of hope.
That perhaps, we're not doomed.
But when you saw the grave that The Void had left ravaged, you could only ask yourself...
How much time do we have? Just how fast are we seeing lives and entire communities be erased without a chance to fight back?
Maybe perhaps once it's all gone, this is what will remain. Or what has always been there.
''A crumbling world,''
''Souls obliviously lost,''
''And a perfect emptiness...''
''Left behind by an '''Abyssal Decay'''''.
A slow surrender to the monotony that once founded our trinity.
{{Blurquote|image=Void.png|quote-text=
''"And if you gaze long enough into an abyss, the abyss will gaze back into you."''|person=Friedrich Nietzsche}}
'''''Level 860:'''''
[[File:860rwtitle2.png|890px|center]]
{{Class Custom Image|bg-image=Voidcombs2.jpg|hexagon=↯|safety=Inconsistent Safety|security=Decaying Stability|entity=Non-Entity Hazards|class=Deteriorating|header=Threat Index: Class}}
  ✎ '''Overview'''
{{Blurimage|file=860NEW.png|caption=A photograph of Level 860.|hovertext=Ruin.|align=center}} '''Level 860''' resembles an abandoned, old-fashioned Southern-American suburban neighborhood styled after the late 1930s. The streets are long and arranged in a grid-like pattern, lined with numerous residential homes. Each house is visibly aged, featuring chipped paint, damaged roofing, and overgrown yards. While they can be entered, they appear lifeless, dilapidated, and empty. While streetlights are present beside the sidewalks, they frequently malfunction, causing inconsistent lighting in the level and even potential blackouts. The houses within the level are mostly confirmed to be abandoned, with interiors typically containing dust-covered furniture and nonfunctional utilities. '''Level 860''' is characterized by its unique case of [[Nullification]], which has been referred to as '''The 860 Effect'''. '''Level 860''' hosts a drastically slow variant of nullification that has caused it to be in a constant state of deterioration. As a result, fissures open to [[The Void]] are present throughout the level manifested as pure black cracks that spread, [[Object 300|Subvoid Fragments]] are more common to find, and the level has continued to decrease in stability. {{Blurimage|file=860.webp|caption=A photograph of The Oblivion Household. Photographer missing.|hovertext=Sanctuary.|align=center}} Located at the center of the level lies '''The Oblivion Household''', a large mansion surrounded by an open grassy clearing. The structure is enclosed by an iron gate that remains unlocked at all times. The surrounding area consists of a maintained lawn, a fenced backyard, and a compact garden area containing several paved stone pathways. In contrast to the general decay of the surrounding residential zone, the mansion exhibits minimal structural degradation and appears to retain operational lighting and limited utilities. Current evidence supports that the building is the only active structure within the level, being inhabited by the '''Oblivio Family'''. Outside the neighborhood, which spans approximately 3 square miles, the level dissipates into an open grassy field, with hills and depressions across the landscape. The farther one goes out, the more disorganized and "repetitive" these systems get. Eventually, one would reach a barrier, pitch black and seemingly peering into '''The Void'''. '''Level 860''', being rumored to have once been habitable, bears remnants that may indicate a more peaceful past. The level, despite its ruined landscape and dangerous safety hazards, has plenty of resources, territory, and a lack of dangerous entities. It is unknown at which period in time '''Level 860''' faced nullification, but since nullification is not bound to The Backrooms, it is probable that '''Level 860''' has only continued to worsen. Due to its structural instability, environmental inconsistency, and high probability of exposure to [[The Void]], '''Level 860''' is rendered near-uninhabitable, and it is expected to only get worse. It is best to avoid any entrance to '''Level 860''' unless belonging to a documentation or exploration team with the proper resources, Using the level as a means of harvesting resources is unreliable due to the constant presence of nullification.
  ✎ '''Hazards'''
'''Level 860''' itself has no hazards; however, its state of deterioration due to the effects of [[Nullification|mass nullification]] has brought along many hazards, some being lethal. Large fissures into The Void and any unstable objects such as [[Object 300|Subvoid Fragments]] are lethal, and any contact with them is unadvised unless prepared. Additionally, given the level's instability, it is not ideal to traverse it without proper materials, as one would risk Nullification or spontaneous noclipping. ==The 860 Effect== The '''860 Effect''' is an abnormal and highly prolonged instance of a '''Nullification Standstill''', observed only in '''Level 860'''. Unlike standard cases, which only delay the process of '''Nullification''', '''The 860 Effect''' slows it down to a point where it appears frozen. That being said, '''The Void''' still remains active in the level, consuming at a rate that is nearly imperceptible to the naked eye. Although the process is still ongoing in the level, the rate of decay in '''Level 860''' is considered the slowest to ever be recorded. Trace reports and evidence suggest the level's boundaries were much larger many years ago, suggesting that it has been undergoing gradual '''Nullification''' for decades. Nobody knows how this phenomenon exists on such a scale. The commonly accepted theory for most '''Nullification Standstills''', the reduction of the rate of entropy, is likely a contributing factor. However, the persistence and strength of '''The 860 Effect''' suggest other factors at play. Some believe the level, due to its increasing instability, possesses several temporal anomalies that may slow the passage of time for certain processes. Said anomalies do not alter the perception of time for wanderers, but appear to slow the flow of '''The Void's''' influence. Time inside and outside the level appears to continue normally. This phenomenon has had multiple effects on the level. As implied, the level exists in a constant state of decay, which is evident by the dilapidated architecture of the neighborhood and the absent sky, which has been replaced by a dark, bluish-black. ==The Farlands== '''The Farlands''' is the designation for the outer grasslands that surround the neighborhood of '''Level 860''', serving as the transitional boundary between the main level and '''The Void'''. As previously noted, '''The Void''' manifests here as a massive, pitch-black wall that borders the level on all sides, continuing to advance at an extremely slow but steady rate. Terrain within '''The Farlands''' becomes increasingly unstable the farther one travels from the neighborhood. Hills that initially appear gentle may become unnaturally steep or jagged, sometimes forming sharp, angular ridges that do not match the surrounding terrain. Depressions may also distort into elongated trenches, and other features like rocky outcroppings may repeat themselves. '''The Farlands''' terminate at the barrier surrounding the level. Any attempt to approach close within it dramatically increases risk of [[Nullification]]. ==Graveshadows== {{Blurimage|file=shadowfromthegrave.jpg|caption=A Graveshadow sighting within a house in Level 860.|hovertext=Dead memory|align=center}} '''Graveshadows''' are entities that reside and have only been found in '''Level 860'''. They are shadowy, humanoid silhouettes that are unstable, having inconsistent proportions, unusual movements such as staggering or contorting, and a notable buzz noise emitting from them akin to a broken VHS tape. '''Graveshadows''' at first had no clear origin, but interviews with the '''Oblivio Family''' have confirmed that '''Graveshadows''' are completely non-sentient fragments left behind from Facelings within '''Level 860''' whom have perished due to [[Nullification]]. '''Graveshadows''' will engage in behaviors akin to a regular civilian. They will walk across the pavement, follow traffic rules even if the street is desolate, operate objects such as bicycles or less commonly vehicles, and go into homes to do indoor activities as well. Interacting with them is impossible, as they do not respond to any external actions or contact, and harming them in any way is impossible. '''Graveshadows''' are referred to by the '''Oblivio Family''' as their "neighbors", and the family will even sometimes call out to them, mentioning their "names". '''Graveshadows''' after a period of time will naturally disappear. They will eventually either walk out into the streets and fade away, noclip and disappear, or randomly freeze in place before dissipating. '''Graveshadows''', while not inherently hazardous, have been associated with many mental health issues that wanderers face after leaving '''Level 860'''. As such, it is best to avoid them in any way and remain in place if they come near you, and avoid questioning any members of the '''Oblivio Family''' about them.
  ✎ '''The Oblivio Family'''
{{Blurimage|file=arm860house.webp|caption=A Bedroom.|hovertext=Where Dreams Come To Disappear.|align=center}} As aforementioned, '''The Oblivion Household''' is the only surviving structure in the level that is still inhabited. It is a mansion whose architecture is styled after the 1930s. It contains standard rooms one would find in a mansion, such as a kitchen, living room, bedroom, etc. The kitchen has a fridge in which food somehow regenerates through unknown means, possibly from '''The Void's''' influence. The fridge contains common items like '''Almond Water''' and '''Moth jelly'''. At night, the lights abruptly turn off, plunging the household into darkness. {{Blurimage|file=Attvoid860p.png|caption=The Attic.|hovertext=Beyond Recognition.|align=center}} Small zones of '''Nullification''' are present throughout the mansion, though most instances are small, under 10 square feet. That being said, they cause slight distortion and visual warping to the area around them. For example, paintings near these instances witness their images becoming distorted. One area of the mansion, the attic, is sealed off from access due to it being partially consumed by '''The Void'''. The mansion is inhabited by a group collectively referred to as '''The Oblivio Family''', consisting of four Facelings identified as the final remaining residents of '''Level 860'''. Each member demonstrates stable humanoid form and consistent behavioral patterns. Each member of the family is as follows: *Dan(44)-The father. Oftentimes, he is welcoming towards wanderers, offering them food, resources, and even a chance to stay in the house. However, they do not talk much. *Mary(41)-The mother. She is strict, but is loving and caring to her children, and outside wanderers alike. *James(14)-The son. He is energetic, but possesses snarkiness around other wanderers. *Amy(7)-The daughter. She is the youngest of the family and is soft spoken. She is often very shy, and in the presence of wanderers, typically stays in her room/ The family demonstrates consistent hospitality, offering food, shelter, and conversation to wanderers. The household, having self replenishing resources and a relatively sustained living condition compared to the rest of the level, is the safest point in '''Level 860'''. However, the mansion isn't completely safe. It still is affected by Nullification, albeit in a lesser rate, and wanderers could still risk lethal exposure to '''The Void'''. ==Anomalies== Despite the family's hospitality, they seem to be in a delusional state of normalcy. They are completely unaware of '''The Void''' and the condition of '''Level 860'''. Discussion or presentation of evidence relating to the structural decay or the presence of '''The Void''' results in denial or confusion. The family collectively dismisses such phenomena as “pest markings” or minor maintenance issues. A similar case is seen between them and the '''Graveshadows''', with members of the family remaining dissonant to any anomalous activity in '''Level 860'''. Repeated questioning of the family's members has often resulted in the wanderers involved being kicked out. Despite being seemingly normal Facelings, the members of the family are unnaturally strong, and as such, any attempts to detain them for further questioning have been ineffective. The reasons behind the apparent obliviousness of the '''Oblivio Family''' can only be attributed to anomalies within '''Level 860'''. Often suggested theories explain the anomalies with 'mental degradation caused by exposure to The Void', or even the intervention of entities. No affirmative conclusion has been made to explain the nature of the '''Oblivio Family'''. The '''Oblivion Household''' remains the only remotely safe space in '''Level 860''', and as such, it is the most secure place to go should any wanderer enter '''Level 860'''. It is suggested to avoid provoking any members of the '''Oblivio Family''', both for safety reasons and to avoid being exposed to the dangers of '''Level 860'''.
  ✎ '''Addendum'''
---- An excerpt of an interview log was found, detailing the conversation of V.C.U. founder Thatcher, with Dan, Subject 860-1, the father of the '''Oblivio Family'''. {{Collapse|num=20|initial=
'''>Open Log<'''
|final=
'''Close Log'''
|body=
'''V.C.U. INTERVIEW 860'''
'''27th of November, 2025''' --------------------------- [THATCHER]: Hello, Mr. Oblivio. [DAN]: Greetings. What's the matter? [THATCHER]: Nothing urgent. We’re simply conducting a small conversation. We need to gather information about Level 860’s properties and peculiarities. [DAN]: Ah. I see. I’m proud you recognize the charm found in these humble residences. Ask away. [THATCHER]: Are you aware of other organizations operating within The Backrooms? [DAN]: Of course. In fact, I’ve seen many of them come here and try to set camp. Well... at least before. They’d come here very often with their notepads, and sometimes they’d even try to set up their own residences downtown. Can’t say I approved of all the noise, but they’ve never come since a long time ago. I can’t help but wonder why. [THATCHER]: I see. And how long have you been receiving other Wanderers? [DAN]: Oh, but they’ve always been coming! At first, back when this town was still young, it was strange to have guests all the time, but we gladly allowed them in. Some would be more… desperate, and others calmer. Some of them would come in strange suits, though—askin’ questions, borrowin’ food, takin’ pictures. I’m not aware of what happens beyond this small residence, so I usually try to stay silent to avoid trouble. [THATCHER]: And have you ever had to resort to violence in case of other wanderers entering your home? [THATCHER]: Dan? Hello? ---------- ''Note: Interview paused temporarily. Subject 860-1 exhibited prolonged silence, followed by a blank, unblinking stare directed toward the recorder. All environmental microphones detected a brief static interference lasting 4.2 seconds before normal audio levels resumed. Interview reconvened after approximately seven minutes.'' ---------- [THATCHER]: Okay. We’re back on setup. [DAN]: Sorry about the delay. I’ve been zonin’ out lately. [THATCHER]: That’s fine. Let’s continue. Are you aware of the anomalies or dangers that The Backrooms present? [DAN]: The Backrooms? [THATCHER]: The Backrooms, the place you're currently located in. [DAN]: Oh, right. Ehh… I’m not exactly an expert, and I’ve never gone anywhere beyond town, so I can’t say me or my family know much about that nonsense. [THATCHER]: “Nonsense”? You mean entities, environmental hazards, or— [DAN]: —That stuff. Just rumors, I’d say. We’ve got no problems here, and the neighbors haven’t complained in years. [subject chuckles softly] It’s quiet here. Always has been. All the noise comes from the pigeons and rodents... [THATCHER]: You mentioned “neighbors.” Can you clarify who you’re referring to? [DAN]: The folks down the road, of course. The Burtons, the Crawleys, the O’Neills... nice people. Don’t come by much anymore, though. Busy lives, I imagine. [THATCHER]: Mr. Dan, are you aware that those residences have been confirmed to be empty and in states of decay? [DAN]: [pause, faint static detected] I don’t think that’s right. You must’ve gone at a bad hour. People here like to keep to themselves. [THATCHER]: …Right. Moving on—does the name “The Void” mean anything to you? [DAN]: “The Void”? [pause] No, can’t say it does. Is that another group like yours? I’ve told your friends before, we don’t get much company these days. [THATCHER]: It’s not an organization, Dan. It’s a spatial phenomenon. Cracks, distortions—black surfaces that expand and consume structures. You haven’t noticed anything like that around your home? [DAN]: [long pause, microphone distortion increases] Can’t say I have. We take good care of our home here. The walls are fine. Just a few marks from pests every now and then, but nothing serious. [THATCHER]: Pests. I see. Do these “marks” ever spread? [DAN]: Hmm… sometimes they look different in the mornings, but Mary cleans them up before breakfast. No harm done. [THATCHER]: Understood. And your family—do they ever leave the house or explore the neighborhood? [DAN]: Leave? Why would we? Everything we need is right here. The lights, the food, the warmth. Mary doesn’t like the kids wandering out at night anyway. Says the air’s colder now. [THATCHER]: When you say “colder,” do you mean temperature fluctuations? [DAN]: No. It’s... a different kind of cold. Hard to explain. You’ll feel it if you stay too long. [THATCHER] ...Understood. Those were all the questions for today. I hope you, and your family, have a nice evening. [DAN]: ...
'''END LOG'''
Following the interview, an abrupt rise in instability forced Thatcher's team to retreat to Base Equilirbium. Attempts to interrogate the other members of the Oblivio Family were constantly intercepted by Subject 860-1, claiming his family has no business with the other organizations. }} -----------------------------
  ✎ '''Entrances & Exits'''
===Entrances=== * Cracks or sinkholes found in the middle of nowhere in [[Level 10]] will bring you here. * Touching a small patch of grass in [[Vacuum Cycle]] will teleport you to this location. ===Exits=== * Noclipping inside ''The Oblivion Household'' will send you to [[Level 193]]. * Breaking through a wall in the attic of ''The Oblivion Household'' and jumping through it will lead you to [[The Attic]]. * Upon exiting certain houses within the neighborhood, you will enter [[Level 11]].

File is classified under the [[V.C.U.]] database. Please provide your authentication to view this file.
{{User:PayourTAXES769/VCUlogin2|body=
[[File:PaperRot.png|link=|2000px]]
=Level 860, “The Old Town”= [[File:Pre860.png|380px|center|link= ]] ------------------------- - This is the earliest documentation of Level 860 found. It was used by the '''V.C.U.''' as means of understanding the properties of Nullification Standstills overtime. It was published in 1944 by '''Walter Hamilton''', a World War II soldier that joined M.E.G. after entering The Backrooms and served for 3 more years before retiring. He lived the rest of his years in '''Level 69''' before passing away in 1997 due to a fatal case of '''Hydrolitis Plague'''. ------------------------- '''I was sent on a last expedition for the Major Exploration Group. In reality, I was just looking for a place to choose for retirement, with how dangerous this realm is. Then, I stumbled across a humble town. I thought it might be good for me, but I've noted many oddities. I decided to document this on my own accord as my last work.''' '''''Level 860'' presents itself as a modest rural settlement, not unlike the small townships seen in the American Southwest some decades back. The homes are simple wood-frame structures with wide porches and pale fencing. The sky holds a steady late-afternoon glow, with no sun or cloud to speak of, and the air is still in a way that is noticeable but not immediately unsettling. The dirt roads are kept in decent shape and lined with cut grass and the occasional tree. The town feels lived-in, though curiously quiet given the number of houses.''' '''During my time here, I met several families who claimed to be longtime residents. They were polite, if somewhat distant, and all seemed unused to receiving visitors. The Burtons live in a blue house on the eastern bend. Mr. Burton offered me a seat and a glass of water, though the water carried a faint metallic flavor he didn’t seem to notice. They reportedly keep chickens, but I saw none—only heard them somewhere behind the house. Mrs. Burton mentioned the birds “wander off sometimes.”''' '''The Crawley couple, elderly and pleasant, live in a cream-colored home decorated with flowerboxes. Their carnations looked wilted, yet both insisted they had been blooming just fine. They said people in the town had been staying inside more, though did not elaborate further.''' '''The O’Neills live near the southern path. A young couple, they spoke kindly, but both looked tired, as if they had not slept properly in some time. They told me their children were sleeping indoors, but the house was silent throughout my visit.''' '''At the center of town is the Dan residence. The Dan family greeted me warmly. Mr. Dan seemed pleased to have conversation. The rest of the family was present but quiet, watching me with a manner that felt more observant than social, though this may simply be an unfamiliarity with outsiders.''' '''Life in the town appears to follow simple rural habits. Many houses have vegetable patches, though I could not determine if the plants grow or change at all. Lamps remained lit inside homes, even with the constant daylight. The town lacks the usual sounds of a community—no animals, no wind, no tools—only a faint electrical hum that did not seem to originate from any visible power source. I found no stores or public buildings, though several residents mentioned “trading” with one another. Clocks in the homes all displayed different times, and each resident insisted theirs was correct enough for daily needs.''' '''Moving outward toward the edges of the settlement, the grass thins and the ground turns dry and cracked. The air becomes colder—not a natural chill, but a hollow sort of cold that settles into the lungs. At the farthest point I reached, I found thin black fissures in the ground and along fences. They looked like cracks in stone, yet appeared on dirt, on wood, and, in certain light, even across open air. Their widths shifted slightly as if breathing. When I held my torch against one, the beam vanished entirely, swallowed without reflection. A soft static could be heard when standing close to these cracks, fading once I stepped away toward town. I decided to name these places the "farlands". I can't tell if this is an anomaly, but for the better of anyone reading this, it is best to stay far from them.''' '''The residents denied knowing anything about these formations. Asking about them made several individuals grow uncomfortable, and conversations were quickly redirected. I've reported this to the database.''' '''The level itself is safe, though I suspect there might be underlying psychological hazards that affect both the civilians and wanderers in Level 860.''' '''Though calm, the place feels as though it is gradually hollowing from its edges inward. The families continue on, quietly and politely, seeming unaware—or unwilling to acknowledge—that the land around them is changing.''' ------------------------------ Filed by Expedition Leader Walter J. Hamilton
End of Report — 1944
}} }} {{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{User:AstralRose555/quote|fontsize=20|Vanity of vanities, saith the Preacher, vanity of vanities; all is vanity|Ecclesiastes 1:2|color=#ffffff}}
'''You see it too, now, don't you?'''
'''This world. These innocent lives, all doomed to perish.'''
'''But not to The Void, ''no.'''''
'''''But to their own ignorance.'''''
'''This will continue on and on... for who knows how long?'''
'''Weeks? Months? Years? ''Eons?'''''
'''And to their last seconds, as the world crumbles around them, they will spit their denial in face of the abyss.'''
'''''To never be seen again.'''''
'''But why? What am I trying to show you with this?'''
'''I'm showing you a ''mirror.'''''
'''A mirror of how humanity drives itself into an endless cycle of self destruction.'''
'''All because of ignorance.'''
'''And the powerless victims desperately searching for a meaning amongst the chaos will wander around like shadows.'''
'''Forever bound to the dark.'''
'''But this is one of many outcomes, you see.'''
'''Demise can also came at one's very own hands.'''
'''War, beliefs, religion, personal desires...'''
'''But this is different.'''
'''Because nobody can control it.'''
'''Not even I, or itself.'''
'''And as far within the boundless reaches of my mind I know...'''
'''That's how it is going to be until the end.''' '''So now, ask yourself.''' {{Collapse|num=3|initial=Out of all the outcomes...|final='''''Which will it be?'''''|body=
=='''''
[[User:PayourTAXES769|Taxi]]

Author's Note: This level is part of both the Ineterna and Main Canon, but has some differences: on the Main Canon, it is simply a town affected by slowed Nullification with the Oblivio Family remaining the same. The level retains its backstory and its hazards and entities. The Ineterna version keeps all of the other fancy canon details. }} {{Audio|mono=yes|round=yes|file=860new.mp3|align=left|volume=0.6|=yes|loop=yes|secrettext=abyss}} [[Category:Ineterna]] [[Category:Dangerous]] [[Category:Levels]]
